changelog:
# Changelog

## 2026-04-22 (Mission Progression + Streak Bonus)

### New Features
- Added a mission progression system with daily and weekly objectives, including per-period reset windows, claim tracking, and mission rewards.
- Added a new authenticated endpoint `POST /api/profile/missions/claim` so players can claim completed mission rewards directly from the profile.
- Added ranked streak bonus rewards that scale with consecutive wins and are included in match-end progression payloads.

### Improvements
- Extended profile payloads to always include mission state (`daily`, `weekly`, summary, and available claims) for immediate frontend rendering.
- Extended ranked match reward flow to return richer progression data (base reward, streak bonus, and mission delta snapshot).
- Extended `game_finished` socket payloads with `progression_updates` so match-end UI can show mission/streak progress without extra polling.

### Frontend
- Added mission claim support to `AuthContext` and wired profile mission cards with progress bars and claim buttons.
- Added match-end progression cards in the Game Over screen for mission status, newly completed missions, and streak bonus details.

## 2026-04-21 (Launcher Motion Polish + R6 Variety Pass)

### Improvements
- Smoothed the launcher main shell by reducing the heavy constant ambient/tilt motion and softening the primary status and info cards so the desktop launcher feels less choppy on real hardware.
- Expanded the seeded R6 starter pool to six distinct reveal groups per map and updated the round selection logic so each R6 sequence favors more different-looking images instead of near-identical cuts.
- Switched the starter `Kanal` and `Outback` R6 sources to interior-style official images so the default R6 experience stays closer to indoor site recognition.

## 2026-04-21 (R6 Interior Starter Pack Refresh)

### Improvements
- Replaced the old R6 blueprint starter pool with official Ubisoft interior-style map imagery so the default R6 mode now feels closer to real in-map site recognition instead of floorplan study.
- Rebuilt the seeded R6 starter pack to cover both `old` and `new` map eras with multiple reveal variants per map, giving the existing R6 mode a stronger ready-to-play base.

## 2026-04-21 (R6 Panel Size Pass)

### Improvements
- Shrunk the R6 answer/sequence panel in the lower-right HUD so it no longer covers such a large part of the screen during play.
- Tightened the R6 answer buttons and sequence card spacing for a cleaner, more compact tactical layout.

## 2026-04-21 (R6 Sequence Preload Fix)

### Fixes
- Fixed the R6 flash sequence so it now preloads the entire screenshot set before the timed reveal starts, preventing rounds from ending while images are still loading.
- Added a dedicated R6 loading state that shows screenshot load progress before the sequence begins.

## 2026-04-21 (R6 Starter Pack + Metadata Tools)

### New Features
- Added a seeded R6 starter location pack built from official Ubisoft blueprint map images, covering 9 maps and both `old` and `new` eras so the R6 mode has a strong playable base immediately.
- Added a reusable `backend/scripts/seed_r6_starter_pack.py` tool that rebuilds the starter R6 pool and refreshes the built-in R6 mode defaults in one pass.

### Improvements
- Extended the admin location tools so R6 locations can now be created and edited with `map_name`, `map_key`, and `era` directly in the dashboard.
- Extended bulk location import so R6 imports can also set default `map_name`, `map_key`, and `era` values without hand-editing the database.
- Improved the admin location table so R6 entries visibly show their map metadata at a glance.

## 2026-04-21 (Launcher Auto Update)

### New Features
- Added a live launcher updater path that now checks the public installer feed on startup and can download the newest launcher installer automatically from GitHub.
- Added release artifact generation for updater publishing, including a ready `version.json` manifest and `SHA256` checksum next to the built installer under `release/`.

### Improvements
- Switched the packaged launcher updater config to the public installer feed so player installs can discover future launcher releases automatically.

### Fixes
- Removed the superadmin self-test ranked crate shortcut from the profile/inventory flow so the live build no longer exposes that debug grant action.

## 2026-04-20 (Render Deploy Prep)

### New Features
- Added a ready-to-sync `render.yaml` blueprint for deploying the backend to Render as a Web Service with a persistent disk.
- Added a dedicated `/api/health` backend endpoint for Render health checks and zero-downtime deploys.
- Added Render deployment docs and a Render-specific environment template for the backend.

## 2026-04-20 (Release Prep + Remote Launcher Mode)

### New Features
- Added a dedicated `launcher-config.json` pipeline so the packaged launcher can run in either `local` or `remote` backend mode.
- Added a player-facing installer build flow that outputs a single distributable setup file under `release/StreetGuess-SA-Installer.exe`.
- Added publishing documentation and a ready remote launcher config example for live deployment prep.

### Improvements
- Improved the launcher UI so it now shows whether it is using a local backend runtime or a remote live backend target.
- Improved the installer packaging flow with desktop shortcut, Start Menu shortcut, launcher icon usage, and install-directory selection.

### Fixes
- Removed account/test credential regeneration behavior that could leak or reset default admin login information during startup.
- Cleaned out old temp assets, legacy test uploads, and outdated release leftovers from the packaged client flow.

## 2026-04-17 (3D Character Skin Foundations)

### New Features
- Extended the skin metadata model so shop skins can now describe real 3D-character-ready assets, including body type, species, archetype, skeleton, asset format, and dedicated 3D model paths.
- Improved skin preview UI across shop, profile, and showroom components to surface whether a skin is humanoid, animal-based, and marked as `3D Ready`.
- Added two sample 3D-ready skin entries (`Operator Zero` and `Street Lynx`) plus example asset manifests under `frontend/public/assets/skins/` to establish the new asset pipeline direction for real characters instead of card-style placeholders.

## 2026-04-14 (Achievement System)

### New Features
- Added a persistent player achievement system with automatic unlock tracking for ranked play, wins, level progression, shop collecting, score milestones, coin milestones, and daily reward usage.
- Added achievement data directly into player profiles so the system works across login, daily reward claim, ranked reward payout, shop purchase, and admin progression updates.
- Added a new achievement panel to the profile menu showing unlocked state, progress bars, milestone targets, and unlock dates.

## 2026-04-14 (Bulk Location Import)

### New Features
- Added bulk location import for admins so multiple screenshot locations can be created at once instead of filling coordinates one-by-one.
- Added optional manifest-based import support with `.csv` or `.json` metadata using `filename,name,coord_x,coord_y`.
- Added automatic filename parsing for coordinates using the pattern `Name__X__Y.jpg`, so imports can work even without a separate manifest file.

## 2026-04-14 (MTA Location Generator)

### New Features
- Added a new MTA `location_generator` resource for automated screenshot collection while moving around the GTA SA world.
- Added timed auto-capture commands, clean-screen mode, coordinate capture, and per-session CSV manifest generation.
- Added output that is designed to feed directly into the admin bulk location import workflow.

## 2026-04-14 (Critical Skin + R6 Stabilization)

### Fixes
- Fixed skin creation end-to-end by adding optional `preview_file` upload support to `POST /api/admin/shop/skins`.
